title: Date Difference Calculator
description: Calculate the difference between two dates in days, weeks, months, and years. Great for planning and deadline tracking.

ui.hintBoth: Enter start date and end date

howToUse.label: Enter start date
howToUse.description: Enter the starting date. Click 'Today' to quickly fill in today's date.

howToUse.label: View results
howToUse.description: The difference in days, weeks, months, and years is calculated automatically. Use the copy buttons to grab any value.

faq.question: How do I find a date X days from now?
faq.answer: Set the start date to today using the 'Today' button, then enter your target date as the end date to see the number of days between them.

faq.question: How are months and years calculated?
faq.answer: Months and years are calculated on a calendar basis, not by fixed day counts. For example, Jan 1 to Mar 31 is 2 months and 30 days.

faq.question: Can I use dates in the past?
faq.answer: Yes. The calculator always returns the absolute difference, so you can enter dates in any order.

faq.question: Is this useful for project management?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Use it to calculate sprint lengths, project durations, or deadline countdowns. The week display is especially useful for sprint planning.
